title: Female-Powered Comedy With ‘Rough Night’ Creators Lucia Aniello and Paul W. Downs | The Big Picture (Ep. 16)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Ringer writer Lindsay Zoladz about profiling director Lucia Aniello, then sits down with Aniello and Paul W. Downs on writing Rough Night — the first R-rated studio comedy directed by a woman since 1998.

films.title: Rough Night
films.note: Aniello's studio debut, and the peg for Zoladz's Ringer profile.

films.title: Half Baked
films.note: Tamra Davis directed it — the last R-rated studio comedy by a woman before Rough Night, and an influence Aniello names.

films.title: CB4
films.note: Also Tamra Davis, who came up cutting music videos.

films.title: The Decline of Western Civilization
films.note: Penelope Spheeris's punk-underground documentary; Aniello cites her as a role model.

films.title: Wayne's World
films.note: Spheeris's jump from documentaries to a big studio comedy.

films.title: Pitch Perfect 2
films.note: Elizabeth Banks-directed and lucrative, but pitched at a PG-13 audience — the line Aniello wanted to cross.

films.title: Sisters
films.note: Fey and Poehler's feature; Zoladz reads it as a throwback to wanting the big studio vehicle.

segments.heading: The Rough Night conversation
segments.guest: Lucia Aniello & Paul W. Downs on

films.title: Rough Night
films.note: Bachelorette weekend, an accidentally dead stripper. Written on spec in the Obama era, released under Trump barely changed.

films.title: The Hangover
films.note: One of the male-centric comparisons the pair keep getting; they'd rather people just watch the movie.

films.title: Bridesmaids
films.note: Fennessey's example of the same outsized expectation that lands on any comedy by women.
